home.title: Home
home.subtitle: Cloud accounts
home.heroTitle: Connect accounts and view all files like a Drive interface.
home.heroDesc: Sync metadata from real cloud accounts and display file list in a Google Drive-like interface.

drive.title: My Drive
drive.dragDropTitle: Drag, drop, and stream directly to the target cloud
drive.dragDropDesc: File payloads pass through the Node.js proxy, while metadata is mirrored locally and progress is reported by WebSocket.
drive.noFiles: No files in this virtual path.
drive.deleteConfirmDesc: Item will be moved to trash.
drive.metadataDescription: Metadata from provider and local mirror.

quota.title: Storage
quota.disconnectConfirmDesc: Account will be removed from OmniCloud but data in the original cloud remains safe.

help.title: OmniCloud Quick Help
help.subtitle: A brief summary to help you understand OmniCloud's main workflow faster.
help.createFolderDesc: Use the New button to create a new folder directly from active storage.
help.uploadFilesDesc: Upload files or folders and monitor progress through the app's process notifications.
help.searchFilesDesc: Use the search box at the top to browse files in OmniCloud.
usageTipsList: Use the sidebar menu to switch between home, drive, and storage overview.
Click the OmniCloud logo on desktop to open the creator profile and open source project info.
Press the Esc button to close any open dialog.
help.connectAccount: How to connect a cloud account
connectAccountSteps: Open the Storage page, then press the Connect button to select a provider like Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox, or MEGA.
Then follow the login or authorization process according to the provider. If the account is disconnected, suspended, or the token is no longer valid, use the Connect button to try reconnecting.
After successfully connected, the storage capacity and files from that account will automatically appear in OmniCloud.
help.howItWorksStep1: You connect multiple cloud accounts from various providers into one OmniCloud dashboard.
help.howItWorksStep2: The app will sync file metadata and storage capacity so all accounts can be viewed from one place.
help.howItWorksStep3: When you create a folder, upload, rename, or delete, OmniCloud will forward that action to the related provider and display the progress in the interface.
help.howItWorksStep4: This way, OmniCloud serves as a unified management layer for many cloud accounts in a neater experience.
